matches.x: Stand fast in this liberty] The Apostle Paul admonished the Galatian Saints to "stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage" (Galatians 5:1). The similarity of language suggests that the expression of both men may have been influenced by an earlier text or prophetic oracle. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L.…

The thought frequently arises in my mind, are we as a people honest and sincere in the professions we make? Do we prove by our dealings, our acts and conversations, that we sincerely believe in all of the principles of the Gospel which we have been willing to preach to others; or do we sometimes in our weakness, preach one thing and practice another? Do we manifest more of the fruits of the flesh…

Stand fast in this liberty] The Apostle Paul admonished the Galatian Saints to "stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage" (Galatians 5:1). The similarity of language suggests that the expression of both men may have been influenced by an earlier text or prophetic oracle. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L.…

Perfect liberty, the free power of choice, is found only were there is adherence to gospel principles. The gospel is "the perfect law of liberty." (Jas. 1:25; 2:12.) By accepting the gospel, men become partakers of "the glorious liberty of the children of God" (Rom. 8:21), liberty from the bondage of sin, the darkness of error, the lusts of the flesh. "Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is…

The thought frequently arises in my mind, are we as a people honest and sincere in the professions we make? Do we prove by our dealings, our acts and conversations, that we sincerely believe in all of the principles of the Gospel which we have been willing to preach to others; or do we sometimes in our weakness, preach one thing and practice another? Do we manifest more of the fruits of the flesh…

For behold, ye are free] This freedom is not something inherent in man. It comes through the Atonement. Lehi taught: 'The Messiah cometh in the fulness of time, that he may redeem the children of men from the fall. And because that they are redeemed from the, fall they have become free forever, knowing good from evil; to act for themselves and not to be acted upon." (2 Nephi 2:26, italics added.)…

I believe these are promises to the nations in which we reside. When the people of God withheld their tithes and offerings, Malachi condemned “this whole nation” (Malachi 3:9). Similarly, I believe that when many citizens of a nation are faithful in the payment of tithes, they summon the blessings of heaven upon their entire nation. The Bible teaches that “righteousness exalteth a nation”…

Love is the essence of the gospel and the greatest of all the commandments. The Savior taught that every other commandment and prophetic teaching hangs upon it. 5 The Apostle Paul wrote that "all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself." 6 (Joseph B. The scriptures list many of these "peaceable[fruit[s] of righteousness" (Heb. 12:11; 2 Cor. 9:10). Paul wrote that the "fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance" (Gal. 5:22-23). He further explained, "For the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth .... Have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of…

'I am the vine,' Jesus stated at the Last Supper, 'ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing' (John 15:5). To abide in Christ is to trust in him, rely on him, be patient with him and his plan for us, yield ourselves to his mercies, surrender to him, and thereby draw upon his life and his strength. missionary journeys: "I purposed to come unto you . . . that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among other Gentiles." (Rom. 1:13.) Paul taught that "we should bring forth fruit unto God" (Rom. 7:4), and he labeled and defined what some of the fruits should be: "The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith" (Gal. 5:22).
